title: Security Spec Frontmatter
description: Validates the YAML frontmatter of specs/security.md. SecurityAgent reads these fields at every gate. The secrets_in_code_allowed field is a hard constraint: it must always be false. Any spec that sets it to true will be rejected by the validator.

version.description: Semantic version of this spec document (e.g. '1.0.0').

threat_model.description: Identifier or short description of the threat model applied (e.g. 'STRIDE', 'OWASP-API-2023', 'custom-v1'). Must be non-empty to confirm the model has been defined.

owasp_top10_reviewed.description: Confirms that the OWASP Top 10 (2021) has been reviewed and each item mapped to a mitigation or explicitly accepted as not applicable.

secrets_in_code_allowed.description: Hard security constraint. MUST be false. Secrets (credentials, API keys, tokens) in source code are never permitted. The validator will reject any spec where this field is true.

pentest_required.description: Optional. Whether an external penetration test is required before production launch.

validated.description: Whether the frontmatter has been validated by tools/validate-specs.py.

validated_at.description: ISO 8601 datetime of the last successful validation.
